qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Incorrect unserialize option key 'allowedClasses' in FileSpool bypasses class restriction enabling PHP object injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns insecure deserialization due to a typo in the unserialize option key in FileSpool.php (typo3/sysext/core/Classes/Mail/FileSpool.php). The advisory describes missing authorization in the recycler module (typo3/cms-recycler) allowing unauthorized table data deletion. These are different components, different vulnerability classes (CWE-502 vs CWE-862), and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes missing authorization in the TYPO3 recycler module (CWE-862), where backend users with recycler access can delete data from any TCA table regardless of table-level permissions. The sole qualifying finding concerns insecure deserialization in FileSpool.php due to a typo in the unserialize option key — a different component, different vulnerability class (CWE-502 vs CWE-862), and different root cause.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-862 (Missing Authorization) in typo3/cms-recycler affecting table-level delete permissions.
filespool-allowedClasses-typo concerns CWE-502 (Insecure Deserialization) in typo3/sysext/core/Classes/Mail/FileSpool.php - different component and vulnerability class.
